title: viola × ryoniae
intro: Viola × ryoniae, also known as the hybrid pansy, is a cross between the common pansy (Viola x wittrockiana) and the horned pansy (Viola cornuta), resulting in a unique and adaptable plant. The leaves and flowers of Viola × ryoniae have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Viola × ryoniae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
